project_summary: Config-driven health monitor and remediation agent for any factory. Two binaries (supervisor + fixer) with Ed25519 signed protocol. Supervisor polls HTTP endpoints and detects failures. Fixer listens on Unix socket, verifies signed instructions, executes remediation with rate limits. Drop-in component for single-machine factory deployments.
target_users: Makers running factories who need health monitoring without setting up Prometheus/Grafana. Any factory using the Dark Factory pipeline.
tech_stack: Go 1.23, Ed25519 (stdlib only), Unix domain sockets, systemd
budget: Home tokens only — zero cloud cost
acceptance_criteria: A maker can configure N health check URLs and M services to restart, run both binaries, and observe automatic detection and remediation of a stopped service — with full Ed25519 signed audit trail.

components.name: protocol
components.description: Ed25519 identity management and signed message types (Finding, Instruction, ActionResult)
responsibilities: Generate and load Ed25519 key pairs
Sign and verify Findings, Instructions, and ActionResults

components.name: observer
components.description: Polling loop that runs configured health checks and dispatches findings
responsibilities: Poll HTTP health endpoints at configurable interval
Send signed Instructions to Fixer via Unix socket

components.name: fixer
components.description: Unix socket listener that verifies and executes signed remediation instructions
responsibilities: Verify Ed25519 signature on incoming Instructions
Execute remediation actions with rate limiting

components.name: config
components.description: JSON configuration with environment variable expansion
responsibilities: Apply sensible defaults for missing values

functional_requirements.title: Config-driven HTTP health checks
functional_requirements.description: Supervisor polls a configurable list of HTTP URLs. Each check has a name, URL, timeout, and optional remediation action. Failures create signed Findings.

functional_requirements.title: Ed25519 signed protocol
functional_requirements.description: Every Finding, Instruction, and ActionResult is signed by the sender's Ed25519 key. Fixer only accepts Instructions signed by the trusted Supervisor key.

functional_requirements.title: Unix socket communication
functional_requirements.description: Supervisor sends Instructions to Fixer via Unix domain socket. Newline-delimited JSON. No network exposure.

functional_requirements.title: Rate-limited remediation
functional_requirements.description: Fixer enforces per-service restart cooldowns and per-job requeue limits. Blocked actions return error ActionResults.

functional_requirements.title: Service restart action
functional_requirements.description: Fixer can restart local systemd services via systemctl. Only the services listed in config are restartable.

functional_requirements.title: Alert escalation
functional_requirements.description: When automated remediation is exhausted or unavailable, Fixer dispatches alerts via log file and optional webhook.

non_functional_requirements.description: Hard separation: Supervisor never mutates state, Fixer never observes. All instructions must be cryptographically signed. Untrusted signers are rejected.
Zero external dependencies beyond Go stdlib. Single JSON config file. Two binaries. No database required.
Both binaries must be restartable by systemd (Restart=always). Stale Unix sockets cleaned up on start.

constraints.description: Go, stdlib only (no third-party deps). Single binary per role.
Must run on a single Linux machine with systemd. No container runtime required.

out_of_scope: Web UI, dashboard, remote SSH actions, TLS certificate checking, database health checks. Those are Dark Factory Sentinel features, not basic sentinel.
